message:
fix: buffer inner-waker stranding and async pool orphaned connection race

Two concurrency bug fixes:

1. service/buffer.rs — BufferFuture::Active completion only woke
   ready_wakers but not inner_wakers, stranding earlier readiness
   waiters that were parked on the inner service becoming available.
   When two siblings shared a buffer and the first completed, the
   second's inner_waker was never fired, causing it to hang
   indefinitely.  Fix: drain and wake inner_wakers alongside
   ready_wakers on normal completion.  New test
   active_completion_wakes_all_inner_readiness_waiters covers the
   two-sibling regression case.

2. database/pool.rs — AsyncDbPool::try_get() could orphan a
   connection if the pool was closed while the connection was held
   outside the lock for validation.  After validation, the code
   pushed the connection back to the idle pool without rechecking
   the closed flag, leaving a live connection in a dead pool that
   would never be cleaned up.  Fix: check closed flag after
   re-acquiring the lock; if closed, disconnect the connection and
   decrement the total count instead of pushing to idle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
